title: You know it would have made more sense if
display_title: You know it would have made more sense if
wikitext:
[]: You know, it would have made more sense if             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:12 am Adam really was the name of humanity's progenitor.. and Lilith was the mother of "angels". Why they chose to make humanity into the Lilin/Nephilim, I'm not sure. Other than coming from the black moon, the "Lilith" moniker barely fits the entity.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:39 am adam came first. If humans were the true succesors of earth, there goes one big plit device. The angels wouldnt be fighting back, they'd actually be invading. Eww cliche

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:56 am Then just swap the arrival orders. Lilith makes first contact, prepares to propagate, and Adam steals her thunder. Although, Evas being made from Lilith wouldn't really make sense (although apparently 01 was actually made from Lilith instead of adam? that plot device is lost on me).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 03, 2009 1:20 am FallenTabris: Humanity being demon-spawn is the opposite of what's expected. You're trying to subvert a subversion, basically.

[]: Re: You know, it would have made more sense if             Quote Post by Synapsid » Fri Jul 03, 2009 1:46 am Reichu you're back We've all missed you, even if you haven't missed us ... and I'm sorry but I've misbehaved while you were away FallenTabris wrote: and Lilith was the mother of "angels". Why they chose to make humanity into the Lilin/Nephilim.

[]: Re: You know, it would have made more sense if             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 03, 2009 7:02 am Synapsid wrote: did the original Jappanese viewers have enougth knowledge of Abrahamic dogma to get it?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:49 pm Lilin, the children of Lilith, are said to count in the millions being born every day while ravaging the world around them in order to subvert Adam's rightful claim to the Earth. Angels, which were ordered by God to bow to Adam as their Earthly father and master, are perfect, immortal, lack human form of any sort, and are known for striking down arrogant monuments of hubris like large cities with giant monster robots. There's little reason to flip things around; this way works too perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Fri Jul 03, 2009 8:38 pm Reichu wrote: Not sure how nephilim entered the conversion; they have nothing to do with Lilith and have more to do with fallen Angels knocking up those always-delectable Earth-women. Or, er, something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 04, 2009 1:14 am "Eve" (Eva) is occupied by the forces of Mother, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sat Jul 04, 2009 1:39 am I mistakenly thought the Nephilim were lilith's spawn as well. Angels, which were ordered by God to bow to Adam as their Earthly father and master, are perfect, immortal, lack human form of any sort, and are known for striking down arrogant monuments of hubris like large cities with giant monster robots.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 04, 2009 2:56 pm Wow, people, the nitpicking over Adam's gender = totally missed I was being metaphorical.

[]:             Quote Post by GoatMan » Sat Jul 04, 2009 3:53 pm The order of Adam landing first actually makes some sense, if you remmeber Adam and the other Angels are associated with the white moon, which is implied to be the moon in orbit around Earth (probably coated in half mile or so thick of random debris, of course, to mask its true form). If this were knocked off the surface of the Earth with Lillith's impact, and Adam were at one point left abandoned in Antarctica during some previous battle with the Lillin, this implies that Adam would have been left with very few options to claim victory. As such, Lillith's children were able to win, and populate the Earth. Even if Lillith ended up off course and landing on the Earth, which was already taken by Adam, the First Ancestral Race obviously had their preference to supporting sentience over immortality, and therefore Lillith's offspring were given the tools necessary to end the conflict with the Lance. The real puzzler is why SEELE believes humanity is at an evolutionary dead end? Evolution is not a discrete process, but something that is a continuous function, which in itself is not a separate entity, but a term we use to describe that order can come from the chaos of random interactions of particles. Granted, this seems completely ridiculous, but even with the development of more complex lifeforms, the universe itself is going from ordered, highly complex energy states to more uniform, lower energy states, as it takes energy to form complex structures. So, given that there is still plenty of energy from the sun, and tons of more material just randomly floating out in space to serve as raw materials to support the Earth, why would humanity be considered to be an evolutionary dead end? As long as there is energy, all lifeforms on the Earth will continue to mutate, and evolve to better increase their chances of their species' survival. Surely ten fingers and toes is not the end all, be all of perfection.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 05, 2009 2:14 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Wow, people, the nitpicking over Adam's gender = totally missed I was being metaphorical.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Jul 05, 2009 4:19 pm Reichu wrote: "Eve" (Eva) is occupied by the forces of Mother, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jul 05, 2009 4:32 pm Goatman wrote: he white moon, which is implied to be the moon in orbit around Earth (probably coated in half mile or so thick of random debris, of course, to mask its true form)

[]:             Quote Post by Tab » Sun Jul 05, 2009 9:19 pm AuraTwilight wrote: They're just being bosnian.
summary:
[]: You know, it would have made more sense if             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:12 am Adam really was the name of humanity's progenitor.. and Lilith was the mother of "angels". Why they chose to make humanity into the Lilin/Nephilim, I'm not sure. Other than coming from the black moon, the "Lilith" moniker barely fits the entity.

[]:          &n
